title: Privileged container DNS resolution issues (with network)
body:
### Issue Description When running priviliged podman with a custom network DNS resolution fails. However, everything is okay when: - I don't create a network and just use the default - running rootless podman ### Steps to reproduce the issue Steps to reproduce the issue 1. sudo podman network create basic-net 2. sudo podman run -d --net basic-net --name fedora fedora:latest sleep infinity 3. sudo podman exec -it fedora curl google.com This is also happening with podman-compose between containers, the above is just a simple reproducer. ### Describe the results you received curl: (6) Could not resolve host: google.com ### Describe the results you expected DNS resolution to work ### podman info output ```yaml host: arch: amd64 buildahVersion: 1.31.2 cgroupControllers: - cpuset - cpu - io - memory - hugetlb - pids - rdma - misc cgroupManager: systemd cgroupVersion: v2 conmon: package: /usr/bin/conmon is owned by conmon 1:2.1.8-1 path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.1.8, commit: 00e08f4a9ca5420de733bf542b930ad58e1a7e7d' cpuUtilization: idlePercent: 97.25 systemPercent: 0.67 userPercent: 2.08 cpus: 8 databaseBackend: boltdb distribution: distribution: arch version: unknown eventLogger: journald freeLocks: 2037 hostname: zissou idMappings: gidmap: null uidmap: null kernel: 6.4.12-arch1-1 linkmode: dynamic logDriver: journald memFree: 24640790528 memTotal: 33350717440 networkBackend: netavark networkBackendInfo: backend: netavark dns: package: /usr/lib/podman/aardvark-dns is owned by aardvark-dns 1.7.0-1 path: /usr/lib/podman/aardvark-dns version: aardvark-dns 1.7.0 package: /usr/lib/podman/netavark is owned by netavark 1.7.0-1 path: /usr/lib/podman/netavark version: netavark 1.7.0 ociRuntime: name: crun package: /usr/bin/crun is owned by crun 1.8.7-1 path: /usr/bin/crun version: |- crun version 1.8.7 commit: 53a9996ce82d1ee818349bdcc64797a1fa0433c4 rundir: /run/crun spec: 1.0.0 +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL os: linux pasta: executable: "" package: "" version: "" remoteSocket: exists: true path: /run/podman/podman.sock security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT rootless: false seccompEnabled: true seccompProfilePath: /etc/containers/seccomp.json selinuxEnabled: false serviceIsRemote: false slirp4netns: executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns package: /usr/bin/slirp4netns is owned by slirp4netns 1.2.1-1 version: |- slirp4netns version 1.2.1 commit: 09e31e92fa3d2a1d3ca261adaeb012c8d75a8194 libslirp: 4.7.0 SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 4 libseccomp: 2.5.4 swapFree: 17179865088 swapTotal: 17179865088 uptime: 0h 35m 9.00s plugins: authorization: null log: - k8s-file - none - passthrough - journald network: - bridge - macvlan - ipvlan volume: - local registries: search: - docker.io store: configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 4 paused: 0 running: 2 stopped: 2 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: overlay.mountopt: nodev graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage graphRootAllocated: 485597708288 graphRootUsed: 279174926336 graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: extfs Native Overlay Diff: "false" Supports d_type: "true" Using metacopy: "true" imageCopyTmpDir: /var/tmp imageStore: number: 86 runRoot: /run/containers/storage transientStore: false volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 4.6.2 Built: 1693343961 BuiltTime: Tue Aug 29 22:19:21 2023 GitCommit: 5db42e86862ef42c59304c38aa583732fd80f178-dirty GoVersion: go1.21.0 Os: linux OsArch: linux/amd64 Version: 4.6.2 ``` ### Podman in a container No ### Privileged Or Rootless Privileged ### Upstream Latest Release Yes ### Additional environment details sudo podman info --format {{.Host.NetworkBackend}}: netavark ### Additional information
--log-level debug output

comments.body: when you run the first container on the network, do you see an aardvark process running on your system?

Yeah, indeed:

```
root       13737  0.0  0.0 1155648 3252 ?        Ssl  Sep22   0:00 /usr/lib/podman/aardvark-dns --config /run/containers/networks/aardvark-dns -p 53 run
```
This is the output from `lsof`:
```
COMMAND     PID USER   FD   TYPE DEVICE SIZE/OFF NODE NAME
aardvark- 13737 root    9u  IPv4 462474      0t0  UDP zissou:domain 
```
Do you see any error logged in journald? Is something else already listening on port 53? You can check with `ss -ulpn`

From the journal, I'm seeing "No configuration found stopping the sever", could that be it? I'm still seeing the process though.
```
Sep 25 10:23:38 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: Received SIGHUP will refresh servers: 1
Sep 25 10:23:38 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: Successfully parsed config
Sep 25 10:23:38 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: Listen v4 ip {"osbuild-net": [10.89.0.1]}
Sep 25 10:23:38 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: Listen v6 ip {}
Sep 25 10:23:38 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: Will Forward dns requests to udp://1.1.1.1:53
Sep 25 10:23:38 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: Starting listen on udp 10.89.0.1:53
Sep 25 10:24:19 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: Received SIGHUP will refresh servers: 1
Sep 25 10:24:19 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: Successfully parsed config
Sep 25 10:24:19 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: Listen v4 ip {"osbuild-net": [10.89.0.1]}
Sep 25 10:24:19 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: Listen v6 ip {}
Sep 25 10:24:19 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: Will Forward dns requests to udp://1.1.1.1:53
Sep 25 10:24:19 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: Starting listen on udp 10.89.0.1:53
Sep 25 14:21:30 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: Received SIGHUP will refresh servers: 1
Sep 25 14:21:30 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: Successfully parsed config
Sep 25 14:21:30 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: Listen v4 ip {"osbuild-net": [10.89.0.1]}
Sep 25 14:21:30 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: Listen v6 ip {}
Sep 25 14:21:30 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: Will Forward dns requests to udp://1.1.1.1:53
Sep 25 14:21:30 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: Starting listen on udp 10.89.0.1:53
Sep 25 14:23:12 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: Received SIGHUP will refresh servers: 1
Sep 25 14:23:12 zissou aardvark-dns[13737]: No configuration found stopping the sever
Sep 25 14:23:45 zissou systemd[1]: Started /usr/lib/podman/aardvark-dns --config /run/containers/networks/aardvark-dns -p 53 run.
Sep 25 14:30:59 zissou systemd[1]: Started /usr/lib/podman/aardvark-dns --config /run/containers/networks/aardvark-dns -p 53 run.
```

This is the output from `ss -ulpn`:
```
State  Recv-Q Send-Q Local Address:Port  Peer Address:PortProcess                          
UNCONN 0      0            0.0.0.0:49648      0.0.0.0:*                                    
UNCONN 0      0        224.0.0.251:5353       0.0.0.0:*    users:(("brave",pid=1287,fd=83))
UNCONN 0      0          10.89.0.1:53         0.0.0.0:*                                    
```
And here is `podman network inspect`, which shows the gateway:
```
[
     {
          "name": "osbuild-net",
          "id": "dce7994838aa8152ec963b0e287454829cb4c1f750a883b1219602d725f754f0",
          "driver": "bridge",
          "network_interface": "podman1",
          "created": "2023-09-22T15:01:15.418190016+01:00",
          "subnets": [
               {
                    "subnet": "10.89.0.0/24",
                    "gateway": "10.89.0.1"
               }
          ],
          "ipv6_enabled": false,
          "internal": false,
          "dns_enabled": true,
          "ipam_options": {
               "driver": "host-local"
          }
     }
]
```

`No configuration found stopping the sever` this is normal assuming all you containers are stopped, the dns sever is only running when it is needed.
Can you show me your firewall setup, how does `iptables -nvL` look like? Do you have a deny all INPUT rule by default?

Ah flip, yeah that's exactly what it was.
```
Status: active
Logging: on (low)
Default: deny (incoming), allow (outgoing), deny (routed)
New profiles: skip  
```
I quickly disabled the firewall to check and it was all good. I'll setup some rules to handle that. Thanks for your help debugging this, I appreciate it.
This should work automatically with the next netavark update: https://github.com/containers/netavark/issues/742
